name: poly_bevel_Q4_grouped_square
description: Polygon Bevel (mesh.bevel, edit-mode Polygons) SQUARE CORNER + GROUP -- task 0458 Phase 3, finding Q4 (2-face group). Two adjacent cube faces (+Y top, +Z front, sharing one ridge edge -- the same pair as poly_bevel_two_faces_grouped.json / poly_bevel_S1_group_segs2.json), inset=0.25, shift=0.15, group=true, square=true, segments=0. Reference topology: 8v/6f -> 22v/16f, a full topology rewrite re-verified bit-exact against the frozen dump poly_bevel_two_faces_grouped_square1.json. The two RIDGE corners (the shared edge's endpoints) get NEITHER split NOR cap -- they stay at their ORIGINAL position, connected directly into the two edge-panels meeting there; the 4 STANDALONE corners (touching only one of the two selected faces) get the normal Q1-style split+cap treatment.
source: Frozen reference-editor capture via the private capture harness's polygon-bevel op (pre-existing dump-oracle poly_bevel_two_faces_grouped_square1.json, re-verified bit-exact under task 0458 -- see toolcards/poly.bevel/findings.md section 3, 'Q4 grouped-2-face square').

provenance.harness: private capture harness (reference-editor polygon-bevel tool, headless scripted op-dump)
provenance.notes: Dump-oracle for square=true + group=true on 2 adjacent faces. Kernel implemented task 0458 Phase 3 (bevelFacesByMask's square param, source/mesh.d) -- a group-shared 'ridge' corner (sharedCornerPos) gets neither a split nor a cap; its neighbourhood is closed by the two edge-panels meeting directly at the original vertex.
